lines.content: * Main entry point for the TradeLog application.
/** Constructs a TradeLog instance with fresh data structures. */
//Handles creating a new Trade and adding it.
* Abstract base class for all commands.
* @param tradeList The current list of trades.
* @param ui        The UI handler for output.
* @param storage   The storage handler for persistence.
public abstract void execute(TradeList tradeList, Ui ui, Storage storage);
* Returns true if this command signals the application to exit.
* Override in ExitCommand to return true.
// Handles finding a trade by index and updating only the provided fields.
// Triggers the final save and closes the loop.
* Command to display all logged trades.
* Corresponds to the user command: {@code list}
* Executes the list command by printing all trades via the UI.
* @param tradeList The current list of trades.
* @param ui        The UI handler used to print the trade list.
* @param storage   Not used by this command.
public void execute(TradeList tradeList, Ui ui, Storage storage) {
//Calls the StatisticsEngine and tells the UI to print the performance metrics.
// A utility class whose sole job is to scan a string like add t/AAPL d/2026-02-18 and
// split it into a map of prefixes and their values.
// Reads the first word (e.g., add, edit) and figures out which command to create.
// Helper methods to safely convert strings into doubles (for prices) or validate the YYYY-MM-DD date format.
// Limits direction strictly to LONG or SHORT
// Limits outcome strictly to WIN or LOSS
// take in a TradeList and calculate your Win Rate, Average Win/Loss, Expected Value (EV), and Total R
// Represents a single trade. It will hold all the variables (ticker, entry, exit, stop loss, etc.).
* Manages the list of trades in memory.
* Handles adding, retrieving, deleting, and sizing of trades.
* Adds a trade to the list.
* Returns the trade at the specified 0-based index.
* Returns the number of trades currently stored.
* @return The size of the trade list.
* Returns true if there are no trades in the list.
* @return true if the list is empty.
* Handles all user interaction output for TradeLog.
/** Prints the welcome banner shown on startup. */
System.out.println("Type 'list' to view trades, 'exit' to quit.");
* Prints all trades in the given TradeList, numbered from 1.
* Prints an empty-list message if there are no trades.
* @param tradeList The list of trades to display.
* Prints an error message to the user.
* @param message The error message to display.
//Handles creating a new TradeTest and adding it.
* Concrete subclass of Command used purely for testing the base class behaviour.
private static class ConcreteCommand extends Command {
public void execute(TradeList tradeList, Ui ui, Storage storage) {
// Handles finding a trade by index and updating only the provided fields.
// Triggers the final save and closes the loop.
String output = captureOutput(() -> command.execute(tradeList, ui, storage));
//Calls the StatisticsEngineTest and tells the UI to print the performance metrics.

// split it into a map of prefixes and their values.

// Limits direction strictly to LONG or SHORT
